verses.text: While Moses tarries, the people make Aaron form a golden calf: 'These be thy gods.' The calf. Impatience breeds idolatry.
While Moses tarries, the people make Aaron form a golden calf: 'These be thy gods.' The calf. Impatience breeds idolatry.
While Moses tarries, the people make Aaron form a golden calf: 'These be thy gods.' The calf. Impatience breeds idolatry.
While Moses tarries, the people make Aaron form a golden calf: 'These be thy gods.' The calf. Impatience breeds idolatry.
They feast and 'rose up to play' before it. Revelry. False worship loosens all restraint.
They feast and 'rose up to play' before it. Revelry. False worship loosens all restraint.
God tells Moses the people have corrupted themselves. Corrupted. Sin grieves the God who saved them.
God tells Moses the people have corrupted themselves. Corrupted. Sin grieves the God who saved them.
He calls them stiffnecked and would consume them, making Moses a great nation. Wrath. God's anger burns against the rebels.
He calls them stiffnecked and would consume them, making Moses a great nation. Wrath. God's anger burns against the rebels.
Moses pleads God's name and His promises to the fathers. Intercession. The mediator stands in the breach.
Moses pleads God's name and His promises to the fathers. Intercession. The mediator stands in the breach.
Moses pleads God's name and His promises to the fathers. Intercession. The mediator stands in the breach.
'And the LORD repented of the evil which he thought to do.' Mercy. God relents at His servant's prayer.
Moses comes down with the tables, 'the writing of God.' The tables. The law was God's own work.
Moses comes down with the tables, 'the writing of God.' The tables. The law was God's own work.
Joshua hears noise; Moses knows it is the sound of singing. The sound. The camp is loud with sin.
Joshua hears noise; Moses knows it is the sound of singing. The sound. The camp is loud with sin.
Seeing the calf, Moses breaks the tables, burns it, and makes them drink its dust. Broken. The covenant is shattered by their sin.
Seeing the calf, Moses breaks the tables, burns it, and makes them drink its dust. Broken. The covenant is shattered by their sin.
Aaron excuses himself feebly: the calf 'came out' of the fire. Excuse. Sin always seeks to shift the blame.
Aaron excuses himself feebly: the calf 'came out' of the fire. Excuse. Sin always seeks to shift the blame.
Aaron excuses himself feebly: the calf 'came out' of the fire. Excuse. Sin always seeks to shift the blame.
Aaron excuses himself feebly: the calf 'came out' of the fire. Excuse. Sin always seeks to shift the blame.
Moses calls, 'Who is on the LORD's side?'; the Levites slay three thousand. Judgment. Sin among God's people is purged.
Moses calls, 'Who is on the LORD's side?'; the Levites slay three thousand. Judgment. Sin among God's people is purged.
Moses calls, 'Who is on the LORD's side?'; the Levites slay three thousand. Judgment. Sin among God's people is purged.
Moses calls, 'Who is on the LORD's side?'; the Levites slay three thousand. Judgment. Sin among God's people is purged.
Moses calls, 'Who is on the LORD's side?'; the Levites slay three thousand. Judgment. Sin among God's people is purged.
Moses returns to God: 'blot me... out of thy book' rather than lose them. Self-sacrifice. The mediator offers himself for the people.
Moses returns to God: 'blot me... out of thy book' rather than lose them. Self-sacrifice. The mediator offers himself for the people.
Moses returns to God: 'blot me... out of thy book' rather than lose them. Self-sacrifice. The mediator offers himself for the people.
God will blot out the sinner himself; He plagues the people. Accountable. Each bears his own guilt.
God will blot out the sinner himself; He plagues the people. Accountable. Each bears his own guilt.
